en.name: Support MPAs effectiveness through strong and connected networks in the Mediterranean
en.description: Despite the increase of Marine Protected Area (MPAs) coverage in the Mediterranean, the objective of maintaining marine biodiversity is far from being met. MPAs are facing several pressures and still lack human capacity, skills and financial resources. Through strong, active and connected networks of MPA managers, the MPA NETWORKS project will help achieve effectively managed MPAs by providing sustainable solutions to challenges requiring a supra-MPA approach: management effectiveness, small scale fisheries management, mobile species conservation and sustainable financing. The project will support testing and adaptation of tools and recommendations from previous projects to improve marine conservation on selected pilot sites and facilitate the transfer of lessons learnt and best practices to the MPA community around the Mediterranean. Networks of MPA managers will be developed and reinforced at national, supra-national and Mediterranean levels, and a regular thematic capacity building programme will be set-up. Networking activities will facilitate exchanges between MPA managers and other actors, capitalisation of good practices and increased knowledge. This approach will be amplified by connecting networks. Through joined forces of networks and a dynamic bottom-up approach linking experience on the ground and decision-making processes, MPA voices will be coordinated and joined recommendations will support policy-making at international, European, and national level.
en.expected_results: 1 9 pilot sites MPAs deliver better management and reduce threats to marine biodiversity building on results from previous EU projects. : The project will enable MPA partners to take over the results of previous EU projects to improve their management activities and find solutions for better marine conservation. They will test, adapt and implement on-site activities in relation with the following key topics: small scale fisheries management, mobile species conservation, sustainable financing, as well as transversal MPA management effectiveness.The activities will be built upon the results and outputs (methodologies, guidelines, tools, recommendations, monitoring protocols, models of governance…) of previous selected Interreg MED, IFM, LIFE or EU projects and other previous work conducted within the MedPAN network. Feedback on lessons learnt, good practices, solutions and recommendations from pilot sites are transferred to other MPAs thanks to the transferring activities of the project.1 9 pilot sites MPAs deliver better management and reduce threats to marine biodiversity building on results from previous EU projects. : The project will enable MPA partners to take over the results of previous EU projects to improve their management activities and find solutions for better marine conservation. They will test, adapt and implement on-site activities in relation with the following key topics: small scale fisheries management, mobile species conservation, sustainable financing, as well as transversal MPA management effectiveness.The activities will be built upon the results and outputs (methodologies, guidelines, tools, recommendations, monitoring protocols, models of governance…) of previous selected Interreg MED, IFM, LIFE or EU projects and other previous work conducted within the MedPAN network. Feedback on lessons learnt, good practices, solutions and recommendations from pilot sites are transferred to other MPAs thanks to the transferring activities of the project.2 Management capacities of MPAs in the Mediterranean are increased through active networking, knowledge sharing and capacity-building on key topics : Mediterranean MPAs will benefit from a large range of tools, expertise and knowledge to improve their management capacities: sharing of good practices, expertise and solutions from pilot sites, experience and knowledge sharing and capitalisation and training programme.These activities will develop MPA skills, and their ability to respond to their local challenges building upon the experiences of other MPAs in the Mediterranean.Thematic working groups will help with transferring activities by dealing with key topics requiring a supra-MPA approach. These working groups will bring together MPA expertise from the Mediterranean to capitalise and bring support to other MPAs. A training programme will also be developed and delivered in MPAs to bring operational, regular and adapted answers to the management needs of Mediterranean MPAs managers.3 Reinforced and connected networks of MPA managers at different geographic scales (national, supra-national, Mediterranean). : Strong and active networks of MPA managers will support the improvement of MPA management effectiveness in the long-term, by increasing knowledge and capacities of MPAs while improving MPA policy and funding thanks to operational and targeted sharing of experience, training and communication between MPAs and towards external targets. At Mediterranean level, the MedPAN network will be reinforced with an improved governance and communication, secured financing plan, improved tools to the benefits of all Mediterranean MPAs. Supra-national and national networks will bring a specific dynamics to the territories, responding to more local MPA challenges with adapted responses. AdriaPAN in the Adriatic sea will evolve towards AdrionPAN to include the Ionian Sea; a network in the Pelagos area will be launched during the project between France, Monaco and Italy. National networks of MPAs will be created or reinforced for transferring and capitalising actions at national level: in Spain (RAMPE) and in France (Forum des AMP), in Croatia (prefiguration of CROMPA).Exchanges and mutual support between networks at all levels will amplify impact and support to MPAs. Links will also be strengthened with other regional networks at Transatlantic level, beyond the Mediterranean to be stronger when bringing the MPA voice at international level.4 Necessary resources for MPA management effectiveness are ensured in post-2020 framework and better integrated by policy-makers at different levels. : A common MPA voice will be raised to provide decision-makers with the knowledge they need to identify the obstacles MPA-related policies implementation sometimes face and to support the improvement of these policies at international, Mediterranean, European and national levels.. Mediterranean recommendations will be developed to support policy-making focusing, within the post 2020 biodiversity framework, on MPA management effectiveness needs and networks of MPA managers added value. Recommendations will be based on the evaluation of the Mediterranean MPA situation (2020 Mediterranean MPA Roadmap and Status Report) and will be discussed during the 2020 Mediterranean MPA Forum (targeting international, European, Mediterranean, national and local authorities). Thematic recommendations will be also produced thanks to the working groups described above. Those Mediterranean recommendations will be communicated during key international and European events (2020 CBD COP and IUCN World Conservation Congress, 2021 Barcelona Convention COP…) as well as at national and local levels for integration in the territorial strategies (national fisheries policies, local maritime spatial planning etc.). Specific recommendations will also be developed for the Adriatic Ionian Macro Region.
en.achievements:
The cooperation among partners was key to ensure the success of the capitalization phase and the good completion of the project within the time frame.Remaining transfer activities were conducted during this last period. Two training sessions on marine turtle conservation and small-scale fisheries and co-management were organized in May 

2022. These sessions were well attended leading to positive evaluations. The training modules developed and tested during the project will be integrated into the MedPAN training permanent catalogue and will be repeated over time after the project. Guidelines on networking have also been elaborated. In total, 82 Mediterranean MPAs were engaged in implementing management strategies during the project.The capitalisation phase was fruitful. A Policy paper was elaborated articulating recommendations at different levels on the five initial topics, plus two new on tourism and climate change. Several national and local capitalisation events were organised to share and discuss recommendations. Dedicated meetings were organised to define collegial recommendations for the EUSAIR strategy. Several activities were also undertaken at European and international levels. Acting as closing meeting, a series of capitalisation events was organised in June 

2022. The three hybrid meetings were well attended and allowed to share widely the lessons learnt and policy recommendations. Five governance plans have been elaborated thanks to a solid collaborative and collective process. A toolbox was delivered on the project website to promote the most useful tools for MPAs on key topics. In terms of contributions to policies, key messages of the project and inputs have been shared and articulated at national, regional, European and international levels. The LP contributed to several key policy and strategic processes promoting the role of networks of MPA managers and the importance of connecting them on key thematic areas. Connections with other regional networks of MPA managers beyond the Mediterranean has also been pursued.The communication supported the capitalization activities at local and regional levels. We collaborated with the MPA ENGAGE project for releasing video pills on key topics for Mediterranean MPAs. Infographics were elaborated during the final capitalisation event to better illustrate the outcomes of the project. The collaboration with MBPC continued, some meetings are planned this fall to promote the results of the project.
en.expected_outputs: Improved management in 9 MPA pilot sites delivering concrete conservation results based on existing outputs from previous EU projects. :40300, a significant number of MPAs benefit from transferring activities and are active in networks :5, joint recommendations on key thematic MPA challenges to be integrated in territorial strategies :5.
en.delivered_outputs: Number of joint governance plans, Surface of habitats supported to attain a better conservation status, Number of protected areas engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in implementing management strategies, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 3,

fr.name: Soutenir l'efficacité des AMP à travers des réseaux solides et connectés en Méditerranée
fr.description: Malgré l'augmentation de la surface d’Aires Marines Protégées (AMP) en Méditerranée, l'objectif de maintien de la biodiversité est loin d'être atteint. Les AMP, confrontées à diverses pressions, manquent encore de capacités humaines et financières et de compétences.Grâce à des réseaux solides, actifs et connectés de gestionnaires d’AMP, le projet MPA NETWORKS contribuera à la gestion efficace des AMP en apportant des solutions durables aux défis nécessitant une approche supra-MPA : efficacité de gestion, gestion de la pêche artisanale, conservation des espèces mobiles et financement durable.Le projet appuiera le test et l'adaptation d'outils et de recommandations de projets antérieurs afin d'améliorer la conservation marine sur des sites pilotes sélectionnés et de faciliter le transfert des leçons apprises et des bonnes pratiques à la communauté des AMP méditerranéennes. Des réseaux de gestionnaires d'AMP actifs seront développés et renforcés aux niveaux national, supranational et méditerranéen, et un programme thématique régulier de renforcement des capacités sera établi. Cette approche sera amplifiée par la connexion des réseaux.Grâce aux forces conjuguées des réseaux et à une approche ascendante dynamique reliant l'expérience sur le terrain et les processus décisionnels, les voix des AMP seront coordonnées et des recommandations conjointes soutiendront l'élaboration des politiques à tous les niveaux.
fr.expected_results: 1 9 AMPs pilotes sont mieux gérées et réduisent les menaces contre la biodiversité, s'appuyant sur les résultats : N/A1 9 AMPs pilotes sont mieux gérées et réduisent les menaces contre la biodiversité, s'appuyant sur les résultats : N/A2 Les capacités de gestion des AMPs en Méditerranée sont améliorées grâce à la mise en réseau, au : NA3 Des réseaux de gestionnaires d'AMP renforcés et connectés à différents niveaux (national, supranational, m&amp : NA4 Les ressources nécessaires pour l'efficacité de gestion des AMPs sont intégrées dans les politiques publiques & : NA
fr.expected_outputs: Meilleure gestion dans 9 AMP pilotes donnant des résultats concrets en matière de conservation, sur la base des résultats de projets précédents. :40300, un nombre significatif d'AMPs bénéficient d'activités de transfert et sont actives dans les réseaux :5, recommandations conjointes sur les enjeux clés pour intégration dans les stratégies territoriales :5.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre de plans de gouvernance communs, Surface d'habitats soutenus pour atteindre un meilleur statut de conservation, Nombre de secteurs protégés engagés (par des chartes, des protocoles, le MoU) dans la mise en œuvre de stratégies de gestion, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 3,

en.name: Ecotourism in Mediterranean Destinations: From Monitoring and Planning to Promotion and Policy Support
en.description:
DestiMED PLUS is the next step on a journey to promote integrated planning in coastal tourism, where regional policymakers apply holistic policies that link tourism with conservation by putting in practice a concrete ICZM approach for ecotourism products in Med Protected Areas (PAs). For ecotourism to thrive, governance and legislation frameworks must be strengthened across the whole Med area. The project builds on the success of MEET and DestiMED, with the objective of supporting Med regions to strengthen the enabling conditions for ecotourism in their PAs. This includes enhancing cross-sectoral local and regional policies, improving integrated planning strategies, and providing stakeholders with effective training and tools to manage, measure, and promote ecotourism. 

DestiMED PLUS will establish a Med Ecotourism Consortium (MEC) of conservation and tourism actors from regional administrations which will work to capitalize on tools and strengthen policy frameworks for ecotourism. The MEC will allow regions to exchange best practices and develop an integrated ecotourism model for the Med that will be adopted by their territories and scaled up. Existing monitoring tools will be harmonized and an online learning platform produced, allowing local and regional authorities to measure sustainability and improve participatory governance, while also ensuring ecotourism benefits conservation in PAs located on islands and in sparsely populated areas.
en.expected_results: 1 Establish a Mediterranean Ecotourism Consortium (MEC) of regions : A Mediterranean Ecotourism Consortium (MEC) gathering the regions participating in the project, representing the interests and the political agenda of both the tourism and conservation departments will be set up. The MEC will: (I) be a place to exchange experiences and jointly work on an integrated approach for planning the sustainable development of regional territories by increasing ecotourism offers while managing protected areas in each region ́s respective territories(ii) Advocate for the inclusion of ICZM approach in coastal tourism policies at EU and Med levels, in addition to regional administrative and PA levels, with a specific focus on islands and sparsely populated areas. The MEC will be formed by the 9 regional project partners, involving administrations from both tourism and conservation. It will remain active after the project ends, within an existing network such as CPMR and NecsTOUR, advocating for integrated sustainable tourism, particularly ecotourism. The MEC will create a space where regions can advocate for integrated policies for tourism and conservation, perfectly addressing the joint and integrated strategies and policies the programme intends to foster.1 Establish a Mediterranean Ecotourism Consortium (MEC) of regions : A Mediterranean Ecotourism Consortium (MEC) gathering the regions participating in the project, representing the interests and the political agenda of both the tourism and conservation departments will be set up. The MEC will: (I) be a place to exchange experiences and jointly work on an integrated approach for planning the sustainable development of regional territories by increasing ecotourism offers while managing protected areas in each region ́s respective territories(ii) Advocate for the inclusion of ICZM approach in coastal tourism policies at EU and Med levels, in addition to regional administrative and PA levels, with a specific focus on islands and sparsely populated areas. The MEC will be formed by the 9 regional project partners, involving administrations from both tourism and conservation. It will remain active after the project ends, within an existing network such as CPMR and NecsTOUR, advocating for integrated sustainable tourism, particularly ecotourism. The MEC will create a space where regions can advocate for integrated policies for tourism and conservation, perfectly addressing the joint and integrated strategies and policies the programme intends to foster.2 DestiMED PLUS Standard for Ecotourism in Med PAs : This result will support Regions and related Protected Areas to holistically measure and monitor the impacts of their actions to foster ecotourism in their territories. The result will capitalize on existing monitoring tools to make them more integrated, holistic, accessible, and user-friendly. This will provide decision makers with better data to prioritize actions and policies that reduce negative impacts and increase the positive contributions of ecotourism. The existing DestiMED standard will be upgraded, and will continue to be a reference framework for ecotourism products in Med protected areas. The result will particularly look at capitalizing DestiMED standard as it will expand the DestiMED´s Ecological Footprint approach by integrating it with a methodology for measuring socio-economic, and conservation indicators. Finally, the project will integrate relevant governance and destination management indicators in the standard, building off the work done in MITOMED+ project and will integrate relevant socio-economic indicators in the standard, building off the work in PEFMED. All these activities will improve the ability of protected areas and regions to holistically monitor the overall impact of ecotourism products in PAs, therefore becoming a functional instrument to measure and improve sustainability in a destination. The result is therefore fully coherent with the programme expectation of applying common indicators for measuring the sustainability of tourism.3 Mediterranean Ecotourism roadmap for regional PAs : Jointly overseen by the conservation and tourism department of each participating region (Regional Ecotourism Coordination tables - RECs), pilot actions will be implemented to test the joint coordination, planning and promotion of ecotourism products in PAs that will lead to an improved enabling environment and long-term viability of ecotourism. Through the joint cooperation scheme between regional conservation and tourism administrations realized via Regional Ecotourism Coordination tables (REC), pilots will test and assess the governance structures that better support the integration of policies, as well as identify common gaps and policy development needs to be synthesized into a policy road map for each region. These will also be integrated into a single Mediterranean policy document at Med level that outlines strategic policy areas to focus on and provides guidelines for policy-makers as to how to act upon them. Specific attention will be given to the functionality of local Public-Private Partnerships and regional cross-sectoral planning. Training modules based on identified target group needs will be offered live and online, and will focus on the ecotourism value chain as a hub for sustainable development, including local and regional governance. These actions will result in 9 destinations having tested a process to improve the integration of their policies through real-life action, while increasing the level of sustainability of ecotourism products taking place in their area. Both sub-results perfectly responding to the TORs expectations.
en.achievements: During the last project semester partners made their efforts in order to complete activities and make for some of the delays experienced in previous periods due to the pandemic-related restrictions. In spite of the tight schedule, the partnership manage to complete most of the foreseen activities, to deliver all major outputs, and to substantially achieve the main objectives of the project. For several deliverables production went beyond expectations.Under WP1, during the first part of the semesters partners managed to ensure the completion of the transition between modules and to proceed to, and prepare for the final project phase.Project communication efforts were intensive, with several tools finalized, such as posters, case study video plus 9 short destination videos, two report cards, ecotourism impact report and ecotourism guidelines for tourism boards. The project was also very active in dissemination efforts: the partnership organized or participated in over 20 events, while familiarisation and press trips were organised in 8 pilot destinations, engaging potential package buyers and media. Under WP3, it was possible to complete the ecotourism package test cycle in all 9 pilot areas involved in the project, with the last protected area (in Lazio) successfuly completing the ecouturism package design and test process successfully. This allowed for testing of the tools developed by the project in all 9 pilot areas.The 5th and final period was marked by the finalisation of the Capitalisation activities (WP5) as well. The ToRs of the Mediterranea Ecotourism Consortium were reviewed and finalized by the partnership under the leadership, coordination and guidance by CPMR. A Policy Roadmap for Ecotourism in the Mediterranean was also drafted, also following a consultation process with partners, associated partners and other key stakeholders. The latter was presented and reviewed by the members of the Intermediterranean Commission during the second meeting of the MEC held on 25 May 2022 online. Both documents were widely presented at the advocacy conference and final project event held on 21 June in Rome. The development of regional action plans was undertaken by most regions. Finally, a good practice database was also finalized, with a questionnaire shared with all the MED regions (especially the IMC/IC member regions) in order to collect good practices in terms of integrated governance of the tourism and conservation departments.
en.expected_outputs: Upgraded Mediterranean Ecotourism standard for products :1, Local Ecotourism clusters :9, Regional Ecotourism Coordination  Tables :9, Upgraded Sustainability Monitoring platform for ecotourism in PAs :1, Protected Area Ecotourism Capacity Building Platform :1, Mediterranean Ecotourism Consortium (MEC) :1, Upgraded Sustainability Monitoring platform for ecotourism in PAs :1, Protected Area Ecotourism Capacity Building Platform :1, Mediterranean Ecotourism Consortium (MEC) :1.
en.delivered_outputs: Number of instruments available to enhance the development of sustainable and responsible tourism, Number of tourist destinations covered by a sustainable tourism evaluation tool, Number of strategies applying sustainable tourism management criteria, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in implementing sustainable tourism plans, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 3,

fr.name: L'écotourisme dans les destinations méditerranéennes: Depuis le suivi et la planification à la promotion et au soutien des politiques
fr.description:
DestiMED PLUS constitue la prochaine étape vers la planification intégrée du tourisme côtier, où les décideurs régionaux appliquent des politiques intégrées liant le tourisme à la conservation. Pour que l'écotourisme puisse prospérer, l'environnement favorable autour de la gouvernance et de la législation doit être renforcé dans l'ensemble de la région méditerranéenne. Le projet s'appuie sur le succès des initiatives MEET et DestiMED, dans le but d'aider régions méditerranéennes à renforcer un environnement propice à l'écotourisme dans leurs aires protégées. Cela comprend l'amélioration des politiques intersectorielles locales et régionales; l’amélioration des stratégies de planification intégrée; la mise à disposition d’une formation et d’outils (pour gérer, mesurer et promouvoir l'écotourisme). 

DestiMED PLUS mettra en place un Consortium Méditerranéen sur l’Écotourisme (MEC) composé d'acteurs de la conservation et du tourisme issus d'administrations régionales. Ce consortium permettra aux régions d’échanger leurs pratiques et d’élaborer un modèle d’écotourisme adopté par leurs territoires et au-delà. Les outils de suivi existants seront harmonisés et une plate-forme d'apprentissage en ligne sera créée permettant aux autorités locales et régionales de mesurer la durabilité de leur territoire et d'améliorer la gouvernance participative, tout en veillant à ce que l'écotourisme profite à la conservation des aires protégées situées sur des îles et dans des zones peu peuplées.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'instruments disponibles pour améliorer le développement de tourisme durable et responsable, Nombre de destinations touristiques couvertes par un outil d'évaluation de tourisme durable, Nombre de stratégies appliquant des critères de gestion de tourisme durables, Nombre de régions et des sous-régions engagées (par des chartes, des protocoles, le MoU) dans la mise en œuvre de plans de tourisme durables, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 3,

fr.name: Intégration de labels durables dans les politiques touristiques méditerranéennes
fr.description:
Face à la nécessité d’un usage responsable des ressources naturelles et culturelles, à la concurrence dans le secteur touristique et aux changements de comportements de consommation, les destinations méditerranéennes sont confrontées à: 

1) Une demande d’amélioration de la qualité de l’offre. 

2) Un besoin de renforcer les liens entre professionnels du tourisme et communautés locales. 

3) La nécessité de communiquer auprès des visiteurs. La certification est un des dispositifs qui répond à ces enjeux et qui démontre l’engagement des destinations à suivre les principes du développement durable. 

Il s’agit d’un outil visant à l’amélioration de l’offre touristique et à la communication. L’objectif du projet est ainsi de développer des mécanismes visant à intégrer des labels durables dans les politiques touristiques, à l’échelle nationale, régionale et locale, et de proposer un appui pour les destinations dans les procédures de certification. Il ne s’agit pas de créer de nouveaux labels, mais de capitaliser sur les certifications existantes. Le projet vise aussi à répondre aux enjeux récents des destinations certifiées : besoin de reconnaissance, complémentarité avec les contenus générés par les utilisateurs et transformation digitale. Ces objectifs seront atteints grâce à des ateliers thématiques d’échange d’expériences, une plateforme en ligne de renforcement de capacités, une politique commune à l’échelle territoriale et un nouveau projet de règlement au niveau du Programme.
fr.expected_outputs: Lignes directrices pour l'organisation des ateliers d'échange d'expériences :1, LABELSCAPE dossier thématique :4, LABELSCAPE plateforme multimodulaire en ligne :1, Rapport sur l'amélioration des politiques existantes (évaluation compris) :8, Memorandum de compréhension signé sur amélioration des politiques territoriales existantes :8, Recommandations politiques pour l'accroissement de la compétitivité du tourisme durable en Méditerranée au travers des labels durables :1.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'instruments disponibles pour améliorer le développement de tourisme durable et responsable, Nombre de destinations touristiques couvertes par un outil d'évaluation de tourisme durable, Nombre de stratégies appliquant des critères de gestion de tourisme durables, Nombre de régions et des sous-régions engagées (par des chartes, des protocoles, le MoU) dans la mise en œuvre de plans de tourisme durables, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 3,

en.name: Integration of sustainability labels into Mediterranean tourism policies
en.description:
Due to the growing need for responsible use of natural and cultural resources, increasing competition in tourism sector and changing consumption patterns, Mediterranean destinations are facing: 

1) Constant demand to improve the quality of tourism offer.

2) Need to strengthen cooperation between tourism providers and local community.

3) Necessity to communicate with the visitors. In sparsely populated, island and protected areas these challenges need to be addressed with greater attention due to specific socio-economic features and increased vulnerability.Tourism sustainability certification is one of the effective instruments which addresses these challenges and shows destination’s commitment to follow the principles of sustainable development. 

It is a tool for improvement of tourist offer, community building and marketing.The overall objective of the LABELSCAPE is thus to develop mechanisms for integrating sustainability labels in wider tourism policies, and to provide support for destination-based initiatives in the certification process. Project capitalises on the existing certification initiatives. It addresses also the latest challenges of certified destinations and businesses: recognisability, complementarity with User Generated Content and digital transformation. LABELSCAPE objectives will be achieved through “Exchange of Experience” workshops, online capacity building platform, policy-change actions in 8 territories and transferable policy recommendations.
en.expected_results: 1 Increased transparency, visibility and usability of sustainability certification in MED tourism destinations : The sustainability label market is fragmented and new relatively small niche schemes and standards are still being created, also in the frame of this Interreg MED programming period (projects SIROCCO, CONSUME-LESS, MITOMED+, DestiMED…). LABELSCAPE aims to collect relevant information about existing labels which correspond to the needs of the Mediterranean coastal tourism destinations for protecting natural and cultural resources, improving destination-level management, contribute to economic and demographic viability etc. By analysing their main characteristics (territorial level, purpose, certification process, key criteria, duration, monitoring) we aim to increase their usability potential and thus facilitate tourism destinations in adopting best certification option. Inclusion of labelling organisations as important project target group will be indispensable for achieving this result.Past projects and existing certification schemes have been testing their own approaches to increase the visibility, both in the side of tourism businesses/tourism authorities and visitors. Experiences form their territorial campaigns are valuable and gathered together within LABELSCAPE project will reach greater number of beneficiaries in Mediterranean and beyond, including key institutional stakeholders in tourism. In the initial phase steps of the LABELSCAPE we will establish links and verify coherence between existing sustainability tourism certification schemes and GSTC standards.1 Increased transparency, visibility and usability of sustainability certification in MED tourism destinations : The sustainability label market is fragmented and new relatively small niche schemes and standards are still being created, also in the frame of this Interreg MED programming period (projects SIROCCO, CONSUME-LESS, MITOMED+, DestiMED…). LABELSCAPE aims to collect relevant information about existing labels which correspond to the needs of the Mediterranean coastal tourism destinations for protecting natural and cultural resources, improving destination-level management, contribute to economic and demographic viability etc. By analysing their main characteristics (territorial level, purpose, certification process, key criteria, duration, monitoring) we aim to increase their usability potential and thus facilitate tourism destinations in adopting best certification option. Inclusion of labelling organisations as important project target group will be indispensable for achieving this result.Past projects and existing certification schemes have been testing their own approaches to increase the visibility, both in the side of tourism businesses/tourism authorities and visitors. Experiences form their territorial campaigns are valuable and gathered together within LABELSCAPE project will reach greater number of beneficiaries in Mediterranean and beyond, including key institutional stakeholders in tourism. In the initial phase steps of the LABELSCAPE we will establish links and verify coherence between existing sustainability tourism certification schemes and GSTC standards.2 Enhanced sustainable management of tourism destinations through use of LABELSCAPE capacity building tools : Sustainability labels, especially the ones designed for destination level, are not only promotional tool, but primarily an instrument for sustainable, inclusive and responsible management of the tourism destinations. They should take into consideration needs and expectations of local community, namely inhabitants and businesses, foreign investors, public authorities (also on higher levels), tour operators, labelling organisations etc. and thus enhance their cooperation. LABELSCAPE capacity building tools will focus on different groups of end-users, but follow the principle of Integrated Coastal Zone Management. Special attention will be given also to the inclusion of tour operators and labelling organisations since the success and effect of sustainability label depends greatly on them. Public authorities, by having power to integrate these instruments in the policies, can decisively facilitate this process.3 Increased recognition of sustainability certification in tourism policies : Examples from all over the Europe show that the motivation of the businesses and destinations to join the sustainability label scheme is much stronger if certification is supported on regional and national level. In LABELSCAPE we aim to firstly collect existing policies and public mechanisms for adopting sustainability labels in Mediterranean coastal tourism destinations (co-funding of certification process, promotion of success stories, making sustainability label preferential criteria for funding, offering support to destinations and local tourism businesses etc.) and share it with interested public via online platform. Next step will be transferring those mechanisms in partner territories via appointed Territorial Working Groups and elaborating policy recommendations with priority fields of actions and priority areas of intervention (on MED and EU level).
en.achievements: Due to the growing need for responsible use of natural and cultural resources, increasing competition in tourism sector and changing consumption patterns, Mediterranean destinations are facing: 1) constant demand to improve the quality of tourism offer, 2) need to strengthen cooperation between tourism providers and local community, and 3) necessity to communicate with the visitors. Tourism sustainability certification is one of the effective instruments which addresses these challenges and shows destination’s commitment to follow the principles of sustainable development. It is a tool for improvement of tourist offer, community building and marketing.In first implementation period LABELSCAPE partnership produced three important results for highlighting the relevance of sustainability labels in wider tourism policies and practices. First result is report "Sustainability standards in tourism" which provides overview of existing sustainability standards in tourism, with special focus on Global Sustainable Tourism Council standards, and explains the logic and importance of third-party auditing process of sustainable practices in tourism. Second result is "Database of active sustainable tourism certifications/labels for Mediterranean destinations, parks, sites and beaches" comprising active, viable and reliable labels in line with GSTC criteria. Third important result is "Methodological guidelines for organising EoE workshops" which introduces different methodologies of participatory processes in the workshops, where participants are more actively involved in the process and therefore more focused on searching solutions for themselves in their respective environment.In the first reporting period project partners prepared factsheets on their territories (where project territorial activities will be implemented) comprising description of development needs, expected policy changes, list of potential stakeholders involved in Territorial Working Group and expected impacts. First LABELSCAPE outputs are available on the project website (https://labelscape.interreg-med.eu), while day-to-day news are published on Twitter account. The presentation of the project in media via articles and press releases has already started. LABELSCAPE project is in full swing for the beginning and all partners are committed to follow project objectives.
en.expected_outputs: Guidelines for organising Exchange of Experience workshops :1, LABELSCAPE thematic packages :4, LABELSCAPE multimodular online platform :1, Report on improvement of existing policies (including evaluation) :8, Memorandums of Understanding for increasing competitiveness of sustainable MED tourism through sustainable tourism labels :8, Policy recommendations on efficient integration of sustainability labels in MED tourism policies :1.
en.delivered_outputs: Number of instruments available to enhance the development of sustainable and responsible tourism, Number of tourist destinations covered by a sustainable tourism evaluation tool, Number of strategies applying sustainable tourism management criteria, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in implementing sustainable tourism plans, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 3,

en.name: Hacking the Mediterranean economy through the Creative and Cultural sector
en.description:
Chebec - Hacking the Mediterranean economy through the Creative and Cultural Sector sector is a 30 months project (Module 2) that gathers 10 full and 5 associate partners from France, Italy, Malta, Portugal, Spain and from the Balkans - Bosnia and Herzegovina and Serbia.
Chebec approach takes inspiration from the fastest boats of the XVIII century used by traders, but also corsairs and pirates fore xplore, exchange, connect and reinvigorate the MED economy.
Chebec tackles CCI main challenge in the Mediterranean: support CCIs access to new markets while keeping their own identity, with the final aim to favor MED clusters attractiveness for creative people. Chebec will experiment an integrated support program for companies and professionals, developing international competences and contacts. The program will include training, consultancy, mobility actions, matching events and an innovation voucher scheme to concretely favour transregional collaborations. Competence building activities will be realised also for policy makers and support organisations to improve the effectiveness of their actions. Outputs will be analysed and transferred at regional levels matching S3 priorities, to ensure Chebec findings last in the long run. Partners are organizations coming from MED regions with relevant CCI clusters, with integrated profiles - public authorities, intermediary organizations, Universities and NGOs - wide experience, solid relations with their regional players and EU networks.
en.expected_results: 1 Reinforced regional ecosystems, connected with other Mediterranean areasMediterranean level : For several reasons, Chebec's partners have verified that a rich and united ecosystem favors the sector of the industries of culture and creativity. Business support operators - public agencies, incubators, institutions, etc. - must develop new skills and close links with their territories or their communities in order to induce new dynamics and launch new initiatives. Links with cluster members from similar regions will enable to develop new knowledge and opportunities for innovation and market development.Chebec will set up matchmaking events that will include companies as well as operators, skill development initiatives via webinars and live seminars as well as the identification of common recommendations for cluster support.These activities, together with an international context - more favourable to exchanges - will help to bridge the gaps in local communities and strengthen collaboration.1 Reinforced regional ecosystems, connected with other Mediterranean areasMediterranean level : For several reasons, Chebec's partners have verified that a rich and united ecosystem favors the sector of the industries of culture and creativity. Business support operators - public agencies, incubators, institutions, etc. - must develop new skills and close links with their territories or their communities in order to induce new dynamics and launch new initiatives. Links with cluster members from similar regions will enable to develop new knowledge and opportunities for innovation and market development.Chebec will set up matchmaking events that will include companies as well as operators, skill development initiatives via webinars and live seminars as well as the identification of common recommendations for cluster support.These activities, together with an international context - more favourable to exchanges - will help to bridge the gaps in local communities and strengthen collaboration.2 Improve CCI companies and professionals capacity to innovate and access Mediterranean markets : Chebec will test a complete program to support trans-regional collaboration among companies and professionals in the cultural industries and creativity industries.A support program will be implemented in the 9 partner regions and will give 10 companies / professionals - in each region - access to a full support service set to develop and implement a growth strategy in other Mediterranean regions .Three mobility actions will be organized to allow the development of collaborations among participants in local support programs."Innovation Vouchers" will support the effective development of trans-regional collaborations.
en.achievements:
The RP started with a networking event involving both partners and beneficiaries: Marseille mobility action.Throughout the semester Partners continued collaborating through the consolidated modus operandi developed in the previous RPs, which have been useful to face the challenges arisen. Flexibility and ad hoc tools were necessary to begin the procedures to request a budget modification and figure out the adjustments of activities in light of the advent of COVID

19.A SCM held in Marseille enabled partners to reflect on the project improvement: the evaluations presented by UVEG indicated the need for the consortium to be dynamic and adapt the methods tested to the peculiarities of CCIs. Partners discussed the option of increasing the sum dedicated to innovation vouchers (I.V) to have greater impacts.The partnership organized an additional technical meeting in Rome: partners took the chance to present the beneficiaries selected for the I.V. scheme in their region and solve challenges and doubts related to their implementation.Partners have been active as far as communication and dissemination are concerned, at a regional, national and international level. The consortium was involved in initiatives organized by the Interreg MED programme and Social & Creative community.AMI participated in the MED for YOU event in Athens. Also, Teatro Ebasko, a beneficiary organization from the Emilia-Romagna was invited to perform at the closing night with a play on the relevance of the European Union.ART-ER attended TALIA’s Mediterranean Forum of Creativity & Social Innovation: the video of the community - featuring a few partners and beneficiaries of the Chebec project - was launched.Other on and offline initiatives were organized by partners locally.Activities developed in the framework of the pilot action continued with different schedules in all regions. In September 2019, beneficiaries had the chance to network during Marseille matchmaking initiative, which was important to build relations in view of the innovation voucher scheme. Partners took part in CIMAC questionnaire providing information on networks from their region: data will be analysed and used to design a map on CCIs clusters.

fr.name: Piratons l'économie méditerranéenne grace à la créativité et la culture
fr.description:
Chebec - Piratons l'économie méditerranéenne grace à la créativité et la culture - est un projet de 30 mois (module 2) rassemblant 10 et 5 partenaires associés de France, Italie, Malte, du Portugal, d'Espagne et des Balkans.
 L'approche Chebec s'inspire du bateau le plus rapide du XVIIIème utilisé par les commerçants et les corsaires pour échanger, relier et revigorer l'économie MED.
Chebec s'attaque au défi majeur des ICC en Méditerranée: aider les ICC à accéder à de nouveaux marchés tout en gardant leur propre identité, dans le but final de soutenir les régions MED à rester attrayantes. Chebec expérimentera un programme de soutien pour entreprises et professionnels, développant des compétences et des contacts internationaux, qui comprendra des actions de formation, de conseil, de mobilité, des événements d'appariement et un système de « innovation vouchers » pour favoriser concrètement les collaborations transrégionales. Des activités de renforcement des compétences viseront également les décideurs et organisations de soutien pour améliorer l'efficacité de leurs actions. Les résultats seront analysés et transférés au niveau régional afin de s'assurer que les résultats de Chebec durent à long terme dans la zone MED et au delà. Les partenaires sont des organisations avec des profils intégrés - autorités publiques, organisations intermédiaires, universités et ONG ayant une grande expérience, de solides relations dans leurs territoires et les réseaux nationaux et européens.
fr.expected_results: 1 Des écosystèmes régionaux renforcés et connectés au niveau méditerranéen : A plusieurs titres, les partenaires de Chebec ont vérifié qu'un écosystème riche et uni favorise le secteur des industires de la culture et de la créativité. Les opérateurs du soutien aux entreprises - agences publiques, incubateurs, institutions etc. - doivent développer de nouvelles compétences et des liens étroits avec leurs territoires ou leurs communautés afin d'induire de nouvelles dynamiques et lancer de nouvelles initiatives. Des liens avec les cluster de régions similaires permettent de développer de nouvelles connaissances et opportunités d'innovation et de marché.Chebec mettra en place des evenements de "matchmaking" qui incluront aussi bien les entreprises que les operateurs, des initiatives de developpement de compétences via des webinar et des seminaires ainsi que l'identification de recommendations communes pour le soutien au secteur.Ces activités ainsi qu'un contexte international - plus propice à l'échange - permettront de souder les liens dans les communautés locales et de renforcer la collaboration.1 Des écosystèmes régionaux renforcés et connectés au niveau méditerranéen : A plusieurs titres, les partenaires de Chebec ont vérifié qu'un écosystème riche et uni favorise le secteur des industires de la culture et de la créativité. Les opérateurs du soutien aux entreprises - agences publiques, incubateurs, institutions etc. - doivent développer de nouvelles compétences et des liens étroits avec leurs territoires ou leurs communautés afin d'induire de nouvelles dynamiques et lancer de nouvelles initiatives. Des liens avec les cluster de régions similaires permettent de développer de nouvelles connaissances et opportunités d'innovation et de marché.Chebec mettra en place des evenements de "matchmaking" qui incluront aussi bien les entreprises que les operateurs, des initiatives de developpement de compétences via des webinar et des seminaires ainsi que l'identification de recommendations communes pour le soutien au secteur.Ces activités ainsi qu'un contexte international - plus propice à l'échange - permettront de souder les liens dans les communautés locales et de renforcer la collaboration.2 Améliorer la capacité des entreprises et des professionnels des ICC à innover et à accéder aux marchés m&amp : Chebec testera un programme complet de soutien à la collaboration trans régionale des entreprises et professionnels du secteur des industries de la culture et de la créativité.Un programme de soutien sera mis en œuvre dans les 9 régions partenaires et pemettra à 10 entreprises / professionnels dans chaque region d'accéder à un ensemble complet de soutien permettant d'élaborer et mettre en œuvre une stratégie de croissance dans d'autres régions méditerranéennes.Trois actions de mobilité seront organisées pour permettre le développement de collaborations parmi les participants aux programmes de soutien locaux.Un système de "Innovation Vouchers" soutiendra le developpement effectif de collaborations transrégionales.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d’instruments opérationnels en faveur de l’innovation des PME, Nombre d'entreprises béneficiant de subventions, Nombre d'entreprises recevant un soutien non-financier, Nombre de clusters d'innovation transnationaux soutenus, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 1,

fr.name: Partage des connaissances pour la rénovation énergétique des bâtiments par les administrations publiques
fr.description:
Le défi territorial principal est d'accélérer la mise en œuvre des directives 2010/31 / UE et 2012/27 / UE concernant le stock existant de bâtiments publics, étant donné les conditions et les difficultés spécifiques liées aux projets du BEE dans la région MED et le potentiel associé des économies d'énergie.
L'objectif global est de renforcer les capacités des administrations publiques pour améliorer l'EE dans leurs bâtiments publics et réduire ses émissions. Les changements attendus peuvent être correctement évalués par des résultats du projet, rentables pour les futurs projets du BEE:
1. Feuille de route pour développer et mettre en œuvre des stratégies de BEE.
2. stratégies BEE régional de soutien mise en œuvre SEAPs.
3. Les accords politiques de BEE régionaux et locaux et les structures de gouvernance.
4. système d'information partagée.
5. Les stratégies de sensibilisation du public, y compris la planification et la mise en œuvre de formations spécifiques dans EEB.
6. combinaison innovante et l'optimisation des modèles financiers BEE.
7. Plan Capitalisation.
8. SHERPA Forum Capitalisation ligne.
9. Capitalisation ensemble de boîtes à outils.
10. Plan d'action conjoint intégrant tous les projets SHERPA BEE.
Transnationalité est crucial depuis de simples mesures locales, régionales ou même nationales ne peuvent pas assurer la coordination et la mise à niveau des acteurs publics avec différentes compétences, l'expérience, le niveau de développement et le potentiel d'amélioration.
fr.expected_outputs: Feuille de route pour développer et mettre en œuvre des stratégies BEE.,Municipalités et les régions au courant des résultats et la méthodologie de SHERPA,Public training strategies, including planning and implementation of specific trainings in EEB,Innovative combination and optimization of financial models for EEB,Shared information system among the participating regions and municipalities,Projects of 100 public buildings to be renovated with EEB measures,EEB projects in public buildings,Joint Action Plan and Final Agreement
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'outils de planification disponibles pour le suivi et la gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de stratégies en faveur des plans de gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de groupes cibles participant aux activités de sensibilisation sur l'efficacité énergétique pour les bâtiments publics, Nombre de régions et sous-régions engagées (par le biais de chartes, protocoles, MoU) à développer des plans/stratégies d'efficacité énergétique, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 2,

en.name: SHared knowledge for Energy renovation in buildings by Public Administrations
en.description:
The main territorial challenge is to accelerate the implementation of EU Directives 2010/31/EU and 2012/27/EU regarding the existing stock of Mediterranean public buildings, given the specific conditions and difficulties related to EEB projects in the MED area and the associated potential of energy saving.
The project overall objective is to reinforce the capacities of Public Administrations at regional and sub-regional level so as to improve EE in their public buildings’ stock and reduce CO2 emissions. The expected changes as a consequence of SHERPA can be properly measured by means of the project outputs, all profitable for future EEB projects:
1.	Roadmap to develop and implement EEB strategies.
2.	Regional EEB strategies supporting SEAPs implementation.
3.	Regional and local EEB Policy Agreements and governance structures.
4.	Shared information system.
5.	Public awareness strategies, including planning and implementation of specific trainings in EEB.
6.	Innovative combination and optimization of EEB financial models.
7.	Capitalisation plan.
8.	SHERPA Online Capitalisation Forum.
9.	Capitalisation set of toolkits.
10.	Joint Action Plan integrating all SHERPA EEB projects.
Transnationality is crucial for SHERPA since merely local, regional or even national measures cannot secure the coordination and levelling of public actors with different skills, experience, development level and improvement potential, but a common framework given by the shared Mediterranean Sea context.
en.expected_results: 1 EEB projects with envisaged 25% energy and 25% CO2 emissions reduction through 300M€ invested in renovation of 100 buildings in the 8 partner reg : EEB projects with envisaged 25% energy and 25% CO2 emissions reduction through 300M€ invested in renovation of 100 buildings in the 8 partner reg : These EEB projects belong to the testing WP and will take place in regional and local governments' public buildings within the partner regions, thereby showing the increase in capacities of Public administrations at regional and sub-regional level within the territories covered by SHERPA, e.g. the project overall objective. The intermediate results related with this main project result, explained with detail within WP4, are: 1. Roadmap document to develop and implement successful EEB strategies. 2. Regional EEB strategies supporting and integrating SEAPs implementation. 3. Regional and local EEB Policy Agreements to properly coordinate measures at the regional and the local level. 4. Shared information system among the participating regions and municipalities. 5. Public awareness strategies, including planning and implementation of specific trainings. 6. Innovative combination and optimization of financial models for EEB. As it can be inferred from the list of the above project outputs, items #2 and #3 are clearly linked with Programme Result Indicator #2 since they increase regional plans including EEB measures for public buildings, whereas item #3 is also linked with Programme Result Indicator #1 since it affects the local level. Clearly, item #1 is a requisite for items #2 and #3, so it is also indirectly connected with programme result indicators #1 and #2. As for items #4, #5 and #6, see Project Results #2 and #3 below, which contribute to Programme Result Indicator #1.1 EEB projects with envisaged 25% energy and 25% CO2 emissions reduction through 300M€ invested in renovation of 100 buildings in the 8 partner reg : EEB projects with envisaged 25% energy and 25% CO2 emissions reduction through 300M€ invested in renovation of 100 buildings in the 8 partner reg : These EEB projects belong to the testing WP and will take place in regional and local governments' public buildings within the partner regions, thereby showing the increase in capacities of Public administrations at regional and sub-regional level within the territories covered by SHERPA, e.g. the project overall objective. The intermediate results related with this main project result, explained with detail within WP4, are: 1. Roadmap document to develop and implement successful EEB strategies. 2. Regional EEB strategies supporting and integrating SEAPs implementation. 3. Regional and local EEB Policy Agreements to properly coordinate measures at the regional and the local level. 4. Shared information system among the participating regions and municipalities. 5. Public awareness strategies, including planning and implementation of specific trainings. 6. Innovative combination and optimization of financial models for EEB. As it can be inferred from the list of the above project outputs, items #2 and #3 are clearly linked with Programme Result Indicator #2 since they increase regional plans including EEB measures for public buildings, whereas item #3 is also linked with Programme Result Indicator #1 since it affects the local level. Clearly, item #1 is a requisite for items #2 and #3, so it is also indirectly connected with programme result indicators #1 and #2. As for items #4, #5 and #6, see Project Results #2 and #3 below, which contribute to Programme Result Indicator #1.2 A total amount of 50 regions and 500 municipalities within SHERPA territories and further across the MED area made aware of SHERPA : A total amount of 50 regions and 500 municipalities within SHERPA territories and further across the MED area made aware of SHERPA : This result will be a consequence of transferring the project results. Intermediate results defined within Project Result #1 above as items #4, #5 and #6 are all necessary elements of the model to be tested within regions and municipalities before it can be transferred and capitalised so as to reach further regions and municipalities and thereby link with programme result indicator #1. Indeed, once the model is properly tested, the transferring process must take place in order to reach as many regions and municipalities as possible within SHERPA territories and further across the MED area. Associated partners of SHERPA will play a key role in this respect since they will seek to maximize the amount of municipalities made aware of SHERPA’S tools and results. However, most probably not all reached regions and municipalities will undertake projects to implement their SEAPs and hence contribute to Programme Result Indicator #1. This will only take place for some regions and municipalities as a consequence of the capitalisation process, described below.3 EEB projects for 100 public buildings developed through the implementation of 140 regional and local EEB Policy Agreements, the latter being SEAPs : EEB projects for 100 public buildings developed through the implementation of 140 regional and local EEB Policy Agreements, the latter being SEAPs : These EEB projects belong to WP6 and will take place (1) in regional governments' public buildings within the associated partner regions and (2) in local governments' public buildings within the partner and the associated partner regions. Hence, this will demonstrate the increase in capacities of Public administrations at regional and sub-regional level within the territories covered by SHERPA, e.g. the project overall objective. The capitalisation process across regions and municipalities within the SHERPA territories (partner and associated partner regions) has as key intermediate results: - Capitalisation plan - SHERPA Online Capitalisation Forum. - Capitalisation set of toolkits containing all relevant outputs of this and other projects regarding EEB projects in public buildings. Obviously, this result directly contributes to Programme Result Indicator #1 since it increases the amount of municipalities being able to implement their SEAPs with EEB measures in public buildings. Furthermore, it also contributes to Programme Result Indicator #2 since associated partner regions will also implement measures in EEB in some of the 100 buildings developed. Moreover, it does not remain an abstract result or a mere agreement, but materialises with concrete EEB projects in public buildings. Finally, all projects developed within SHERPA as a consequence of the testing and the capitalisation phase will be integrated into a Joint Action Plan.
en.achievements:
Since its beginning, SHERPA project has accomplished its most relevant milestones, starting from its Kick-Off meeting in December 2016 in Barcelona, where all partners met, and developed an open event for the Catalan municipalities, where over 100 of them attended and showed interest in the project and its results.Since then, the project's activities have been taking place, and have been properly monitored by the Lead Partner, as well as by the JTS of the MED Programme. The cooperation among the different partners have produced a series of mutually beneficial synergies: For example, the Region of Crete has delegated its participation in the teleconferences, that the project organises to develop its working groups, in the other Greek partner, CRES, which has a wider technical knowledge, whilst the region of Crete leverages the implication of municipalities, on behalf of CRES, in the capitalisation activities.During the first reporting period the project main output produced is O

3.1, "Road Map to EEB Strategies”, including its Preliminary Study, basic to prepare the rest of the testing outputs and produced through the direct cooperation between partners in the frame of SHERPA on line Working Groups and taking into account previous 16 EU projects with complementary objectives. In this sense, the SHERPA road Map to EEB Strategies is the fruit of cooperation and will serve as basis for SHERPA's development. During this 1st period SHERPA has achieved a very important specific objective: Identifying 107 buildings to start the testing phase. Generate this sample of buildings was a very important and basic task developed through the SHERPA online Working Groups (WG). Buildings' identification requires a first level of internal cooperation for each SHERPA Region but also cooperation between partners to establish the terms and criteria to identify them. At the end of the reporting period, SHERPA's Steering Committee approved the selected list with a diversity of buildings used by regional SHERPA partners. During this period partners have started to work in all the rest of outputs foreseen. In WG1 partners are working in Regional EEB strategies (

3.2) and EEB Regional policies (

3.3). WG2 is working in output 

3.4 Shared info system. WG3 is working in output 

3.5 Public awareness and training strategies and have participated in several training sessions and have created a training platform. WG4 has started the discussion about output 

3.6 “Innovative financial models”.

en.delivered_outputs: Number of available planning tools to monitor and manage energy consumption in public buildings, Number of strategies supporting plans on energy consumption management for public buildings, Number of target groups participating in capacity raising activities on energy efficiency for public buildings, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in developing energy efficiency plans/strategies, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 2,

en.name: Mediterranean Innovation STRAtegy for transnational activity of clusters and networks of the Blue Growth
en.description: The current MED area innovation performance in the blue growth (BG) sector is behind the EU average and there is a lack of transnational and transversal cooperation between the different quadruple helix actors in the BG. The aim of MISTRAL is to strenghten a transnational partnership made up of 8 countries (2 Ministries, 8 Regions, 5 Clusters, 3 RTOs and other organizations) in order to: i) make marine knowledge and sustainable innovation the key drivers for BG, ii) support MED clusters to become an excellent intermediary of knowledge for increasing blue economy, iii) design and implement sustainable development trajectories harmonised with the MED regions Smart Specialization Strategies. The strong partnership, thanks to the open innovation approach, will act as catalyzer for cooperative sustainable innovation actions, placing MISTRAL in a unique position to help the boost of the BG sector in the MED area responding to the programme challenges. A clustering innovation service pack for BG clusters and operators will improve the innovation performance of at least 300 SMEs with at least 100 experts benefitting from the transnational activities and exchange programmes on capacity building. MISTRAL has the ambition to develop a wider governance vision towards 2020 in the BG sector as well as assuring the effective policy mainstreaming. In conclusion, MISTRAL will be the Blue Innovation wind in the MED area unlocking the innovation potential of the coastal and marine ecosystem.
en.expected_results: 1 Improve knowledge of MED clusters competencies and capacities : -1 Improve knowledge of MED clusters competencies and capacities : -2 Increase the effectiveness of clusters' services : -3 Development of new value chains models : -4 Global Med Governance : -

fr.name: Stratégie d'innovation méditerranéenne pour l'activité transnationale des cluster et des réseaux de la Blue Growth
fr.description:
La performance d’innovation dans la région MED dans le secteur de la croissance bleue (CB) est derrière la moyenne de l'UE et il y a une manque de coopération transnationale et transversale entre les différents acteurs de la quadruple hélice. MISTRAL a le but de promouvoir et mettre en œuvre un partenariat transnational de 8 pays (2 Ministeres, 8 Régions, 5 Clusters, 3 SRI et des autres organisations) afin de: i)rendre la connaissance et l'innovation durable les principaux moteurs de la CB, ii)soutenir les clusters MED à devenir un excellent intermédiaire de connaissances pour l'économie bleue, iii)mettre en œuvre des trajectoires de développement harmonisées avec les RIS3 des régions MED.
Le partenariat solide aura le rôle de catalyseur pour des actions d'innovation durable, plaçant MISTRAL dans une position unique pour aider à stimuler le secteur de la CB dans la zone MED en réponse aux défis du programme. Un ensemble de service d'innovation pour les clusters et les opérateurs CB améliorera la performance d'innovation d'au moins 300 PME avec au moins 100 experts bénéficiant des activités transnationales et des programmes d'échange sur le renforcement des capacités. MISTRAL a l'ambition de développer une vision de gouvernance plus large vers 2020 dans le secteur de la CB et au même temps assurer une intégration politique efficace. En conclusion, MISTRAL sera le vent de l’économie bleue dans la zone MED débloquant le potentiel d'innovation de l'écosystème côtier et marin.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d’instruments opérationnels en faveur de l’innovation des PME, Nombre d'entreprises béneficiant de subventions, Nombre d'entreprises recevant un soutien non-financier, Nombre de clusters d'innovation transnationaux soutenus, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 1,
